webhookSetup.description: Webhook endpoint requirement (DD113). Declares an endpoint the plugin needs auto-wired at install time.

auth_model.description: Verification method for inbound webhook requests.

external_setup.description: Human-readable instructions for configuring the external service to send webhooks.

help_url.description: URL to external service's webhook configuration page.

fields.description: Credential fields for webhook auth (mirrors setup.fields shape).

help.description: Per-field guidance shown below the input.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text for the input field.

secret.description: true = SecureField in UI, stored encrypted.

name.description: Webhook endpoint name (e.g. xero-invoice). Becomes WebhookConfig.name.

options.description: Processor-specific options (e.g. skill, operator for dispatch processor).

processor.description: Built-in processor type that handles inbound events.

description: Schema for stallari-plugin.yaml — the manifest format for Stallari platform plugins. The legacy filename sidereal-plugin.yaml is also accepted during the Sidereal→Stallari rebrand transition; both resolve to this schema.

author_type.description: Author provenance. first-party = published and maintained by Stallari.

certification.description: Third-party validation and certification state. Tracks the process of promoting community plugins to verified tier.

installs_cleanly.description: Plugin installs without errors via declared runtime.

licence_compatible.description: Licence is compatible (MIT, Apache-2.0, ISC, BSD).

no_data_exfil.description: No unexpected network calls or data exfiltration observed.

tools_callable.description: At least one tool can be called with valid inputs.

tools_enumerate.description: MCP tools/list returns valid tool schemas.

platform_tested.description: Platforms where validation was performed (e.g. macos-arm64, linux-x64).

security_notes.description: Free-form notes on security findings or caveats.

security_review.description: Security review outcome. warn = acceptable with noted caveats.

status.description: Certification workflow status. pending = queued for review, validated = passed checklist, certified = first-party full conformance, revoked = previously validated but withdrawn.

validated_commit.description: Git commit SHA of the validated version.

validated_date.description: ISO date when validation was performed.

validator.description: Who performed the validation (e.g. system-architect, CI).

last_tested.description: ISO date of last conformance run, or null if untested.

contract.description: Domain contract identifier(s). Single string, array of strings for multi-service MCPs, or null for uncontracted tools.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the plugin's capabilities.

default.description: Default value if not set by the user.

description.description: Human-readable description of the variable's purpose.

secret.description: Whether this value should be stored in the system keychain.

icon.description: Relative URL path to icon asset for marketplace display (e.g. '/icons/xero-blade-mcp.svg').

inference.description: Optional declaration of embedded LLM inference. Informational — platform does not enforce or route.

purpose.description: What the inference is used for (e.g. classification, summarisation).

args.description: Additional arguments passed to the command.

header.description: HTTP header name for token injection.

runtime.description: Runtime type for executing the plugin.

url.description: Remote MCP endpoint URL (for runtime = remote).

multi_instance.description: Whether this plugin supports multiple named instances (e.g. multi-site Home Assistant, multi-account email).

name.description: Plugin identifier. Lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens.

notes.description: Free-form notes about the plugin (curation status, caveats, context).

readiness.description: Testing completeness. production = all certification checklist items pass. beta = partially tested. experimental = untested. stub = scaffolded but not functional.

additionalProperties.description: Concrete MCP tool name that implements this operation.

services.description: Service operation mappings. Keys are service names, values map contract operation names to concrete MCP tool names.

setup.description: Setup metadata for credential UI generation, marketplace display, and preflight readiness checks.

auth_model.description: Primary authentication model. Drives harness setup flow selection.

blurb.description: Short marketing copy for the setup wizard (e.g. 'Connect to your Home Assistant instance for device monitoring'). Distinct from help_text which is step-by-step instructions.

complexity.description: Setup effort signal for marketplace. trivial = paste one key, moderate = create app + configure, involved = multi-step OAuth or external setup.

fields.description: Credential fields. Each maps to an environment variable injected at plugin launch.

help.description: Per-field guidance shown below the input (e.g. 'Run make auth to obtain', 'Create at Profile → Security').

key.description: Environment variable name. Should match an entry in the env array.

label.description: Human-readable field label for UI (e.g. 'API Token', 'Store URL').

placeholder.description: Placeholder text for the input field (e.g. 'ghp_...', 'https://store.myshopify.com'). Ignored when type is 'boolean'.

required.description: Whether this field is mandatory for the plugin to function.

secret.description: true = SecureField in UI, stored encrypted. false = plain TextField. Ignored when type is 'boolean'.

type.description: Field type. 'string' renders as text/secure input. 'boolean' renders as checkbox (e.g. write-enable gates).

help.description: Labelled documentation links shown in the setup wizard and settings card (guides, developer consoles, API docs).

help_text.description: Human-readable setup instructions shown in credential wizard.

help_url.description: Link to external setup docs or developer console.

icon.description: SF Symbol name for credential card (e.g. 'cart.fill', 'envelope.fill').

oauth2.description: OAuth2 flow metadata. Present when auth_model is 'oauth2'.

setup_url.description: URL where user creates the OAuth2 app/client.

prerequisites.description: Human-readable external prerequisites (e.g. 'Xero developer account', 'Google Cloud project with Gmail API enabled').

product.description: Service label for credential card subtitle (e.g. 'Admin API Token', 'OAuth2 Client').

setup_guide.description: Relative path to a setup guide markdown file (e.g. 'guides/onedrive-setup.md'). When present, the setup UI shows a step-by-step walkthrough instead of the flat help_text.

test.description: Connection test configuration. Enables 'Test Connection' button in credential UI.

auth_header.description: Custom header name when auth_style is 'header' (e.g. 'X-Shopify-Access-Token').

auth_style.description: How to attach credentials to the test request.

endpoint.description: URL template for connection test. Use {FIELD_KEY} for variable substitution (e.g. '{SHOPIFY_STORE_URL}/admin/api/2024-01/shop.json').

expected_status.description: HTTP status codes that indicate success.

tier.description: DEPRECATED — use author_type + readiness. Legacy quality tier. Certified = first-party, Verified = third-party validated, Community = interest signal. Either tier or both author_type + readiness should be present.

webhooks.description: Webhook endpoint requirements (v1.4, DD113). Declares endpoints the plugin needs wired at install time.

title: Stallari Plugin Manifest
